studies.officialTitle: A Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled, Parallel Group, Multi-Center Phase 2 Study to Evaluate the Efficacy, Safety, and Tolerability of Once-Weekly CT-388 in Participants With Obesity or Overweight With at Least One Weight-Related Comorbidity

design.description: Parallel-group, multi-dose, dose-finding trial of once-weekly subcutaneous enicepatide across five dose levels versus placebo.

studies.population: Adults with obesity, or overweight with at least one weight-related comorbidity, excluding type 2 diabetes.
studies.safetySummary: At ADA 2026, adverse events were predominantly gastrointestinal and mostly mild to moderate; serious and Grade 3 events were infrequent and not dose-dependent, and no Grade 4 or 5 events were reported.

sources.title: A Study of Enicepatide (CT-388) in Participants With Obesity or Overweight With at Least One Weight-Related Comorbidity
Roche announces positive Phase II results for its dual GLP-1/GIP receptor agonist CT-388 in people living with obesity
Roche to present new data advancing its obesity portfolio at the American Diabetes Association's 2026 Scientific Sessions
2813-LB: CT-388, a cAMP Signal-Biased GLP-1/GIP Receptor Agonist, Achieves Clinically Meaningful Weight Loss in People With Overweight/Obesity: A 48-Week Phase 2 Study
Roche ADA 2026 Virtual Investor Event — CT388-103 Phase II results

studies.officialTitle: A Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled, Parallel-Group, Multi-Center Phase 2 Study to Evaluate the Efficacy, Safety, and Tolerability of Once-Weekly CT-388 in Participants Who Are Overweight or Obese With Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus

design.description: Parallel-group, multi-dose trial of once-weekly subcutaneous enicepatide versus placebo in participants with type 2 diabetes; co-primary body-weight and HbA1c objectives.

studies.population: Adults who are overweight or obese with type 2 diabetes mellitus; weight reduction and glycemic control were co-primary objectives.

sources.title: A Study of Enicepatide (CT-388) in Participants Who Are Overweight or Obese With Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus

studies.officialTitle: A Phase III, Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled, Parallel Group Study to Evaluate the Efficacy and Safety of Once-Weekly RO7795068 Administered to Participants With Obesity or Overweight Without Type 2 Diabetes

design.description: Parallel-group pivotal trial of once-weekly subcutaneous enicepatide across three dosing regimens versus placebo.

studies.population: Adults with obesity, or overweight with a weight-related comorbidity, without type 2 diabetes.

sources.title: A Clinical Study to Evaluate the Effects of Enicepatide (RO7795068) in Participants With Obesity or Overweight Without Type 2 Diabetes

studies.officialTitle: A Phase III, Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled, Parallel Group Study to Evaluate the Efficacy and Safety of Once-Weekly RO7795068 Administered to Participants With Obesity or Overweight and Type 2 Diabetes

design.description: Parallel-group pivotal trial of once-weekly subcutaneous enicepatide across three dosing regimens versus placebo in participants with type 2 diabetes.

studies.population: Adults with obesity or overweight and type 2 diabetes.

sources.title: A Clinical Study to Evaluate the Effects of Enicepatide (RO7795068) in Participants With Obesity or Overweight and Type 2 Diabetes

endpoints.name: Percentage change from baseline in body weight
Proportion of participants achieving at least 5% body weight reduction
Proportion of participants achieving at least 10% body weight reduction
Proportion of participants achieving at least 15% body weight reduction
Proportion of participants achieving at least 20% body weight reduction
Proportion of participants achieving at least 25% body weight reduction
Proportion of participants achieving at least 30% body weight reduction
Proportion of participants achieving resolution of obesity (BMI < 30 kg/m^2)
Proportion of participants with baseline prediabetes achieving normoglycemia
